From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

(Redirected from [United Garment Workers' Union](/w/index.php?title=United_Garment_Workers%27_Union&redirect=no "United Garment Workers' Union"))

Former trade union of the United Kingdom

Not to be confused with [United Garment Workers of America](/wiki/United_Garment_Workers_of_America "United Garment Workers of America").

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/e/ec/Tailors%27_Machinists_%26_Pressers%27_Trade_Union_25_Dec_2013.jpg/250px-Tailors%27_Machinists_%26_Pressers%27_Trade_Union_25_Dec_2013.jpg)](/wiki/File:Tailors%27_Machinists_%26_Pressers%27_Trade_Union_25_Dec_2013.jpg)

Offices of the Tailors' Machinists' and Pressers' Trade Union, a forerunner to the UGWTU in [Sheepscar](/wiki/Sheepscar "Sheepscar"), [Leeds](/wiki/Leeds "Leeds").

The **United Garment Workers' Trade Union** (UGWTU) was a [trade union](/wiki/Trade_union "Trade union") in the [United Kingdom](/wiki/United_Kingdom "United Kingdom").

The union was founded in 1915, with the merger of the [Amalgamated Union of Clothiers Operatives](/wiki/Amalgamated_Union_of_Clothiers_Operatives "Amalgamated Union of Clothiers Operatives") with the Amalgamated Jewish Tailors, Machinists and Pressers, the London and Provincial Cutters, the London Jewish Tailors, the [Waterproof Garment Workers' Trade Union](/wiki/Waterproof_Garment_Workers%27_Trade_Union "Waterproof Garment Workers' Trade Union") and the [London Society of Tailors and Tailoresses](/w/index.php?title=London_Society_of_Tailors_and_Tailoresses&action=edit&redlink=1 "London Society of Tailors and Tailoresses (page does not exist)"). The Waterproof Garment Workers soon disaffiliated, but in 1919, the National Amalgamated Shirt, Collar and Jacket Society joined, and membership reached a peak of 102,000.[[1]](#cite_note-1) In 1920, it merged with the [Scottish Operative Tailors and Tailoresses Association](/wiki/Scottish_Operative_Tailors_and_Tailoresses_Association "Scottish Operative Tailors and Tailoresses Association") to form the [National Union of Tailors and Garment Workers](/wiki/National_Union_of_Tailors_and_Garment_Workers "National Union of Tailors and Garment Workers").[[2]](#cite_note-2)

The union's general secretary was [Joseph Young](/wiki/Joseph_Young_(trade_unionist) "Joseph Young (trade unionist)"), its financial secretary was [Moses Sclare](/wiki/Moses_Sclare "Moses Sclare"), and its organiser was [Andrew Conley](/wiki/Andrew_Conley "Andrew Conley").[[3]](#cite_note-marsh-3)
